summary: McKenzie describes a quick UX improvement to his Bingo Card Creator website after his brother couldn't find the download button. He created a more visible download button using open-source clip art and plans to apply similar visual improvements to other trial and buy links across the site. He also notes he's removing links to broken features until they're fixed.
key_points: A real user (his brother) couldn't find the download button, revealing a usability problem
He used open-source clip art to create a more prominent call-to-action button
He plans to add similar visual buttons to all trial and buy links across the site
Broken feature links are being hidden until the features actually work
money_quotes: My little brother, no computer slouch, asked to see my website and couldn't find the download button. Thats a bug, not a feature.
I expect I'll be buttonizing my trial and buy links.
The links for the two things which are currently broken are going away until they're not broken anymore.
key_insight: If a competent user can't find your download button, that's a conversion-killing usability bug that needs an immediate, visible fix.
